laws.title: North Carolina Minimum Wage — Federal Rate Applies ($7.25/hr)
laws.summary: North Carolina's minimum wage matches the federal rate of $7.25/hour. North Carolina is a right-to-work state. Local governments are preempted from setting higher minimum wages. North Carolina has limited state-specific employment law requirements beyond federal law.
compliance_actions: North Carolina follows federal overtime rules (FLSA)
Ensure required federal and NC-specific posters are displayed at NC worksites
Review North Carolina Retaliatory Employment Discrimination Act (REDA) — broader than federal anti-retaliation provisions

laws.title: North Carolina Wage Payment Act — Final Pay Requirements
laws.summary: North Carolina's Wage and Hour Act requires employers to pay all final wages on the next regular payday following termination. Employers must also provide written wage payment policies to employees at hire and post them in the workplace. Unauthorized deductions from wages are prohibited.
compliance_actions: Ensure final paychecks are issued on the next regular payday following termination
Provide written wage and hour policies to all North Carolina employees at hire
Post wage payment policies at NC worksites
Do not make deductions from final pay without written employee authorization (other than legally required deductions)
